methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.0B - $126.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Scale AI, a Series F+ AI & Software company, is evaluated against a peer set of 8 private and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The target's current valuation stands at $29.0B following $15.9B in total capital raised.

Analysis relies on the Peer-Median method, the sole applicable triangulation approach given available data. The methodology identifies 8 same-sector peers at similar valuation tiers, yielding a median valuation of $12.0B. The peer sample includes Waymo ($126.0B, Pre-IPO), Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C), Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), and Crusoe Energy ($10.0B, Series E). The full peer range spans $8.0B to $126.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in business model maturity and market positioning within AI & Software.

The triangulated valuation range is $10.2B (low) to $13.8B (high), with a median of $12.0B. The target's current $29.0B valuation materially exceeds the peer-derived median by 142%, suggesting either valuation inflation relative to comparable stage/sector peers or that Scale AI commands a substantial premium tied to factors not captured in peer multiples—such as proprietary data moat, revenue scale, or path to profitability. Key uncertainties include limited methodological triangulation (single method applied) and the wide dispersion of peer valuations ($8.0B–$126.0B), which constrains confidence in the $12.0B benchmark.
